titleStmt.title: Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister Katie Mooney, 25 Jan 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Peter Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Peter Mooney (b.1886) and his sister Katie were raised by their uncle William and his wife Mary in Caulstown. While there are no surviving enlistment papers for Peter, his War Badge Index Record shows his enlistment date as 18 October 1915. He served with the Royal Artillery and later transferred to the Royal Engineers where he was promoted to the rank of Sergeant. He served in several places at the Western Front, including Ypres, St. Omer and Passchendael. Peter and Kate were avid correspondents. This letter part of a collection of over ninety surviving letters.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ballincollig Jan 25 1916
Dear Katie I thought I would be
home for a few days by this
time. I believe we will not
get leave until next Monday.
However it must come sooner
or later.I will ask you to
send me on some Tobbaco. Tell
all I was asking for them.
I will write and let you know
when I am going home. You must
excuse the paper as I could get
no other. Good Bye & May God Bless
you all. Peter Mooney
Miss K. Mooney
Caulstown
Co. Meath. Dunboyne
